examples.title: SLA Availability Calculator
examples.description: Program to calculate downtime and service credits based on SLA target
examples.code:
def calculate_downtime(availability_percentage, period_hours=8760):
    downtime_hours = period_hours * (100 - availability_percentage) / 100
    return downtime_hours

def calculate_service_credits(actual_availability, monthly_fee, sla_target=99.9):
    # Calculate downtime
    downtime = calculate_downtime(actual_availability)
    
    # Determine credit percentage based on SLA shortfall
    if actual_availability >= sla_target:
        return 0, "No violation - SLA met"
    elif actual_availability >= 99.0:
        credit_percent = 10
    elif actual_availability >= 95.0:
        credit_percent = 25
    else:
        credit_percent = 50
    
    credit_amount = monthly_fee * credit_percent / 100
    return credit_amount, f"{credit_percent}% credit for {downtime:.2f}hrs downtime"

# Example usage
monthly_fee = 10000
actual_availability = 98.5  # Achieved 98.5% availability

credits, message = calculate_service_credits(actual_availability, monthly_fee)
print(f"SLA Analysis: {message}")
print(f"Service credits due: ${credits:.2f}")
examples.explanation: This Python code calculates downtime based on availability percentage and determines service credits according to typical SLA penalty tiers. It demonstrates how to quantify SLA violations financially.

examples.title: SLA Compliance Monitor Pseudocode
examples.description: Pseudocode for monitoring SLA metrics and detecting violations
examples.code:
MONITOR SLA_Compliance(metrics, SLA_Targets)
BEGIN
    WHILE monitoring_active DO
        current_metrics = FETCH_Current_Metrics()
        
        FOR EACH metric IN current_metrics DO
            target = SLA_Targets[metric.name]
            
            IF metric.value < target.threshold THEN
                violation_record = CREATE_Record(
                    metric_name: metric.name,
                    target_value: target.threshold,
                    actual_value: metric.value,
                    timestamp: CURRENT_TIME()
                )
                
                ADD_TO_LOG(violation_record)
                
                IF violation_count >= target.escalation_threshold THEN
                    ESCALATE_TO_SUPPORT(metric, violation_record)
                END IF
                
                IF monthly_violations >= target.credit_trigger THEN
                    CALCULATE_SERVICE_CREDIT(metric, target)
                    NOTIFY_CUSTOMER(service_credit_amount)
                END IF
            END IF
        END FOR
        
        GENERATE_MONTHLY_REPORT()
        SLEEP(monitoring_interval)
    END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the logic for continuous SLA monitoring, including violation tracking, escalation procedures, and service credit calculation when thresholds are breached.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) per monitoring cycle where n is number of metrics
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m is violation history storage

examples.title: RTO/RPO Calculator in C
examples.description: C program to calculate RTO and RPO requirements and recommend backup strategy
examples.explanation: This C program helps organizations select appropriate backup and disaster recovery strategies based on their RTO and RPO requirements. It recommends solutions that meet business recovery objectives within budget constraints.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of strategies evaluated

examples.title: Java SLA Metrics Collector
examples.description: Java class for collecting and analyzing cloud SLA performance metrics
examples.explanation: This Java class implements an SLA metrics collection system that records performance data, calculates averages, and identifies violations against predefined SLA targets. Useful for cloud monitoring dashboards.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of metric recordings
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m is stored metric history
